initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> They offered 50 ZUP, which is above my cost of 40 ZUP. I'm happy to accept this offer as it gives me a profit. I will accept the trade.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>That sounds good, Player BLUE! I accept your offer of 50 ZUP for my X.</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present in the correct order and contain valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP when the cost was 40 ZUP maximizes my profit.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent offered 50 ZUP, so accepting is very likely to be successful.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I am accepting, and the <message> reflects that acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have only used 2 proposals and am now correctly accepting, adhering to the turn constraints.
</critique>
